1014	// TODO(port): std::fs::File needed; stub for Phase A.

1016	    // TODO(port): real implementation reads from a File handle.

1019	
1020	/// Internal chunk reader that returns a single buffer slice then signals EOF.
1021	///
1022	/// C: `static const char *getS(lua_State *L, void *ud, size_t *size)`
1023	fn make_string_reader(data: Vec<u8>) -> impl FnMut() -> Option<Vec<u8>> {
1024	    let mut remaining = Some(data);
1025	    move || remaining.take()
1026	}
1027	
1028	/// Load a file as a Lua chunk. Returns `LUA_OK` on success or an error code.
1029	///
1030	/// C: `LUALIB_API int luaL_loadfilex(lua_State *L, const char *filename, const char *mode)`
1031	// TODO(port): uses std::fs which is banned outside lua-cli; stub for Phase A.
1032	pub fn load_filex(
1033	    state: &mut LuaState,
1034	    filename: Option<&[u8]>,
1035	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1036	) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1037	    // TODO(port): open file with std::fs::File, handle BOM, skip shebang line.
1038	    // For Phase A we always fail cleanly.
1039	    let _ = (filename, mode);
1040	    Err(LuaError::runtime(format_args!(
1041	        "luaL_loadfilex not implemented in Phase A (std::fs banned outside lua-cli)"
1042	    )))
1043	}
1044	
1045	/// Load a buffer as a Lua chunk.
1046	///
1047	/// C: `LUALIB_API int luaL_loadbufferx(lua_State *L, const char *buff, size_t size, const char *name, const char *mode)`
1048	pub fn load_bufferx(
1049	    state: &mut LuaState,
1050	    buff: &[u8],
1051	    name: &[u8],
1052	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1053	) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1054	    // C: LoadS ls; ls.s = buff; ls.size = size;
1055	    // C: return lua_load(L, getS, &ls, name, mode);
1056	    // TODO(phase-b): state.load expects (chunk: &[u8], name, mode) in state_stub; the reader-based loader needs a load_with_reader API match.
1057	    let _reader = make_string_reader(buff.to_vec());
1058	    let ok = state.load(buff, name, mode)?;
1059	    Ok(if ok { 0 } else { 1 })
1060	}
1061	
1062	/// Load a buffer as a Lua chunk (no mode argument).
1063	///
1064	/// C: `#define luaL_loadbuffer(L,s,sz,n) luaL_loadbufferx(L,s,sz,n,NULL)`
1065	pub fn load_buffer(
1066	    state: &mut LuaState,
1067	    buff: &[u8],
1068	    name: &[u8],
1069	) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1070	    load_bufferx(state, buff, name, None)
1071	}
1072	
1073	/// Load a NUL-terminated byte-string as a Lua chunk.
1074	///
1075	/// C: `LUALIB_API int luaL_loadstring(lua_State *L, const char *s)`
1076	pub fn load_string(state: &mut LuaState, s: &[u8]) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1077	    // C: return luaL_loadbuffer(L, s, strlen(s), s);
1078	    load_buffer(state, s, s)
1079	}

3716	
3717	// ── §14 Main function and entry point ────────────────────────────────────────
3718	
3719	/// C: static void mainfunc(LexState *ls, FuncState *fs)
3720	/// Compiles the main chunk (always a vararg function with _ENV upvalue).
3721	fn mainfunc(ls: &mut LexState, state: &mut LuaState, main_fs: FuncState) -> Result<Box<LuaProto>, LuaError> {
3722	    // C: open_func(ls, fs, &bl)
3723	    open_func(ls, state, main_fs)?;
3724	
3725	    // C: setvararg(fs, 0) — main function is always vararg
3726	    setvararg(ls.fs.as_mut().unwrap(), state, 0)?;
3727	
3728	    // C: env = allocupvalue(fs); env->instack=1; env->idx=0; env->kind=VDKREG; env->name=ls->envn
3729	    let env_name = ls.envn.clone();
3730	    {
3731	        let idx = alloc_upvalue(ls.fs.as_mut().unwrap())?;
3732	        let up = &mut ls.fs.as_mut().unwrap().f.upvalues[idx];
3733	        up.instack = true;
3734	        up.idx = 0;
3735	        up.kind = VarKind::Reg.as_u8();
3736	        up.name = env_name.clone();
3737	    }
3738	    // C: luaC_objbarrier(ls->L, fs->f, env->name) — no-op in Phase A
3739	
3740	    // C: luaX_next(ls) — read first token
3741	    lex_next(ls, state)?;
3742	
3743	    statlist(ls, state)?;
3744	
3745	    // C: check(ls, TK_EOS)
3746	    check(ls, TK_EOS)?;
3747	
3748	    close_func(ls, state)
3749	}
3750	
3751	/// C: LClosure *luaY_parser(lua_State *L, ZIO *z, Mbuffer *buff, Dyndata *dyd,
3752	///                           const char *name, int firstchar)
3753	/// Top-level entry point: parses a chunk and returns the main LClosure.
3754	/// LUAI_FUNC visibility.
3755	///
3756	/// PORT NOTE: In C, returns `LClosure *` (a GC object). In Rust (Phase A),
3757	///   we return `Box<LuaProto>` since we don't have GcRef<LuaLClosure> ready.
3758	///   Phase B will wrap this in a proper LuaLClosure / GcRef.
3759	pub fn parse(
3760	    state: &mut LuaState,
3761	    dyd: DynData,
3762	    source: &[u8],
3763	    name: &[u8],
3764	    firstchar: i32,
3765	) -> Result<Box<LuaProto>, LuaError> {
3766	    let source_str = state.intern_str(name)?;
3767	    let envn_str = state.intern_str(lua_lex::LUA_ENV)?;
3768	
3769	    let rest_bytes: Vec<u8> = source.iter().skip(1).copied().collect();
3770	    let z = lua_lex::ZIO::from_bytes(rest_bytes);
3771	
3772	    let lex_ls = lua_lex::LexState {
3773	        current: firstchar,
3774	        linenumber: 1,
3775	        lastline: 1,
3776	        t: lua_lex::Token::eos(),
3777	        lookahead: lua_lex::Token::eos(),
3778	        fs: None,
3779	        z,
3780	        buff: lua_lex::LexBuffer::new(),
3781	        h: None,
3782	        dyd: None,
3783	        source: source_str.0.clone(),
3784	        envn: envn_str.0.clone(),
3785	    };
3786	
3787	    let mut lexstate = LexState {
3788	        current: lex_ls.current,
3789	        linenumber: lex_ls.linenumber,
3790	        lastline: lex_ls.lastline,
3791	        t: LexToken::default(),
3792	        lookahead: LexToken::default(),
3793	        fs: None,
3794	        dyd,
3795	        source: Some(source_str.clone()),
3796	        envn: Some(GcRef(lex_ls.envn.clone())),
3797	        lex: lex_ls,
3798	    };
3799	    // C: luaX_setinput is the only setup the C parser performs before
3800	    //   `mainfunc`; it does NOT pre-read the first token. `mainfunc` itself
3801	    //   issues the initial `luaX_next` once its prelude (open_func, vararg
3802	    //   marker, _ENV upvalue) is in place.
3803	
3804	    let mut main_proto = Box::new(LuaProto::placeholder());
3805	    main_proto.source = Some(source_str);
3806	    main_proto.is_vararg = true;
3807	    let main_fs = FuncState {
3808	        f: main_proto,
3809	        prev: None,
3810	        bl: None,
3811	        pc: 0,
3812	        lasttarget: 0,
3813	        previousline: 0,
3814	        nk: 0,
3815	        np: 0,
3816	        nabslineinfo: 0,
3817	        firstlocal: 0,
3818	        firstlabel: 0,
3819	        ndebugvars: 0,
3820	        nactvar: 0,
3821	        nups: 0,
3822	        freereg: 0,
3823	        iwthabs: 0,
3824	        needclose: false,
3825	    };
3826	
3827	    mainfunc(&mut lexstate, state, main_fs)
3828	}

3832	/// The parser's local `LexState` predates the lex-side enum and uses a flat
3833	/// (r, i, ts) record; this picks out whichever variant the lexer produced.
3834	fn local_token_value(v: &lua_lex::TokenValue) -> TokenValue {

1630	/// Loads and parses a chunk in protected mode, returning the status.
1631	///
1632	/// C: `int luaD_protectedparser(lua_State *L, ZIO *z, const char *name, const char *mode)`
1633	pub(crate) fn protected_parser(
1634	    state: &mut LuaState,
1635	    z: ZIO,
1636	    name: &[u8],
1637	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1638	) -> LuaStatus {
1639	    // C: incnny(L)  — cannot yield during parsing
1640	    // incnny → state.inc_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1641	    state.inc_nny();
1642	
1643	    let mut p = SParser {
1644	        z,
1645	        buff: LexBuffer::new(),
1646	        dyd: DynDataStub::new(),
1647	        mode: mode.map(|m| m.to_vec()),
1648	        name: name.to_vec(),
1649	    };
1650	
1651	    // C: luaZ_initbuffer(L, &p.buff) — LexBuffer::new() already initialised above
1652	    // (macros.tsv: luaZ_initbuffer → buf.init() / Mbuffer::new())
1653	
1654	    // C: status = luaD_pcall(L, f_parser, &p, savestack(L, L->top.p), L->errfunc)
1655	    let top_idx = state.top_idx();
1656	    let errfunc = state.errfunc;
1657	    let status = pcall(state, |s| f_parser(s, &mut p), top_idx, errfunc);
1658	
1659	    // C: luaZ_freebuffer(L, &p.buff) — Rust's Drop handles deallocation (macros.tsv)
1660	    // C: luaM_freearray(L, p.dyd.actvar.arr, ...) — Rust's Drop handles Vec (macros.tsv)
1661	    // (p and all its sub-fields drop here automatically)
1662	
1663	    // C: decnny(L)
1664	    // decnny → state.dec_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1665	    state.dec_nny();
1666	
1667	    status
1668	}
1669	
1670	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1671	// PORT STATUS
1672	//   source:        src/ldo.c  (1029 lines, ~37 functions translated, 2 omitted)
1673	//   target_crate:  lua-vm
1674	//   confidence:    medium
1675	//   todos:         23
1676	//   port_notes:    13
1677	//   unsafe_blocks: 0

1679	//                  setjmp/longjmp → Result<T,LuaError> throughout.
1680	//                  relstack/correctstack omitted (StackIdx already offset-based).
1681	//                  Coroutine functions (lua_resume, lua_yieldk, resume, unroll,
1682	//                  etc.) are translated but require Phase E stack-switching to
1683	//                  actually work.  Hook-callback borrow conflict flagged as
1684	//                  TODO(port) in hook() and finish_ccall(); Phase E must solve.
1685	//                  All method calls (check_stack, gc_check_step, get_ci*,
1686	//                  set_ci*, next_ci, etc.) are best-guess stubs to be wired
1687	//                  up in Phase B once the LuaState API is finalised.
1688	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1689

1560	}
1561	
1562	/// Checks that the chunk mode permits loading the given kind ("binary" or "text").
1563	///
1564	/// C: `static void checkmode(lua_State *L, const char *mode, const char *x)`
1565	fn check_mode(
1566	    state: &mut LuaState,
1567	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1568	    kind: &[u8],
1569	) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
1570	    if let Some(mode_bytes) = mode {
1571	        // C: strchr(mode, x[0]) == NULL  — mode doesn't contain the first letter of kind
1572	        let kind_char = kind[0];
1573	        if !mode_bytes.contains(&kind_char) {
1574	            // C: luaO_pushfstring + luaD_throw(L, LUA_ERRSYNTAX)
1575	            // TODO(port): &[u8] display — lossy UTF-8 here is acceptable for mode/kind
1576	            // strings which are always ASCII literals ("binary"/"text" and "bt"/"b"/"t").
1577	            return Err(LuaError::syntax(format_args!(
1578	                "attempt to load a {} chunk (mode is '{}')",
1579	                core::str::from_utf8(kind).unwrap_or("?"),
1580	                core::str::from_utf8(mode_bytes).unwrap_or("?"),
1581	            )));
1582	        }
1583	    }
1584	    Ok(())
1585	}
1586	
1587	/// Parser callback invoked inside `pcall`: reads the first byte to decide
1588	/// binary vs. text, then calls the undumper or parser accordingly.
1589	///
1590	/// C: `static void f_parser(lua_State *L, void *ud)`
1591	fn f_parser(state: &mut LuaState, p: &mut SParser) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
1592	    // C: int c = zgetc(p->z)  — read first character
1593	    // zgetc → z.getc()  (macros.tsv)
1594	    let c = p.z.getc();
1595	
1596	    // C: if (c == LUA_SIGNATURE[0])
1597	    // LUA_SIGNATURE → const LUA_SIGNATURE: &[u8] = b"\x1bLua"  (macros.tsv)
1598	    let cl = if c == b'\x1b' as i32 {
1599	        // C: checkmode(L, p->mode, "binary")
1600	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"binary")?;
1601	        // C: cl = luaU_undump(L, p->z, p->name)
1602	        // TODO(port): undump returns a LClosure; the Rust API isn't finalised.
1603	        crate::undump::undump(state, &mut p.z, &p.name)?
1604	    } else {
1605	        // C: checkmode(L, p->mode, "text")
1606	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"text")?;
1607	        // C: cl = luaY_parser(L, p->z, &p->buff, &p->dyd, p->name, c)
1608	        // TODO(port): parser API not yet finalised; returns a LClosure.
1609	        parse_stub(state, &mut p.z, &mut p.buff, &mut p.dyd, &p.name, c)?
1610	    };
1611	
1612	    // C: lua_assert(cl->nupvalues == cl->p->sizeupvalues)
1613	    debug_assert!(cl.upvals.len() == cl.proto.upvalues.len());
1614	    // C: luaF_initupvals(L, cl)
1615	    func::init_upvals(state, &cl)?;
1616	
1617	    // PORT NOTE: In C-Lua, `luaY_parser` / `luaU_undump` themselves push the
1618	    // closure onto the stack before returning (see lparser.c `luaY_parser`:
1619	    // `setclLvalue2s(L, L->top.p, cl); luaD_inctop(L);`). In the Rust port
1620	    // they return the closure by value, so `f_parser` must push it here.
1621	    // Without this, the caller (`api::load`) sees stale Nil at top-1 and any
1622	    // subsequent `pcall_k(state, 0, ...)` fails with "attempt to call a nil
1623	    // value".
1624	    state.check_stack(1)?;
1625	    state.push(LuaValue::Function(LuaClosure::Lua(cl)));
1626	
1627	    Ok(())
1628	}

delta.text: Both

tests now pass cleanly.

## Report

**Root cause:** `singleva
raux` in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:1769` had a `TODO(
port)` stub that did NOT recurse into the enclosing `FuncState` chain. When a name (
e.g. `fact` referenced inside its own body) wasn't found as a local or existing upvalue at
the current function level, the code fell through to `Void` (global), so the in
ner closure never registered the upvalue. The bytecode emitter
still wrote a `GETUPVAL` for upvalue 0 referencing `fact`, but the runtime closure was constructed with `up
vals.len() == 0` (because `proto.upvalues.len() == 0`) — `cl
.upvals[0]` then panicked at `state.rs:1300`. Also `singlevar` duplicated the same bro

ken single-level lookup instead of calling the recursive helper.

**Fix:** Restructured `singlevaraux` to properly recurse via
`fs.prev.as_deref_mut()` — when the name is
not local and not an existing upvalue at the current level, it walks up the parent chain, and on unwinding calls `new_upvalue
` at each level if the parent had it as a local or upvalue, mirroring C `
lparser.c`'s `singlevaraux`. Rewrote `singlevar` to `take()` `ls.fs
` out, call the recursive helper, then put it back — sidestepping the previously-

flagged `&LexState` / `&mut FuncState` borrow conflict.

**New status
:** `print(fact(5))` now executes cleanly and prints `120`. Original panic at `state.rs:1300` is gone.

Both tests now pass cleanly.

## Report

**Root cause:** `singlevaraux` in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:1769` had a `TODO(port)` stub that did NOT recurse into the enclosing `FuncState` chain. When a name (e.g. `fact` referenced inside its own body) wasn't found as a local or existing upvalue at the current function level, the code fell through to `Void` (global), so the inner closure never registered the upvalue. The bytecode emitter still wrote a `GETUPVAL` for upvalue 0 referencing `fact`, but the runtime closure was constructed with `upvals.len() == 0` (because `proto.upvalues.len() == 0`) — `cl.upvals[0]` then panicked at `state.rs:1300`. Also `singlevar` duplicated the same broken single-level lookup instead of calling the recursive helper.

**Fix:** Restructured `singlevaraux` to properly recurse via `fs.prev.as_deref_mut()` — when the name is not local and not an existing upvalue at the current level, it walks up the parent chain, and on unwinding calls `new_upvalue` at each level if the parent had it as a local or upvalue, mirroring C `lparser.c`'s `singlevaraux`. Rewrote `singlevar` to `take()` `ls.fs` out, call the recursive helper, then put it back — sidestepping the previously-flagged `&LexState` / `&mut FuncState` borrow conflict.

**New status:** `print(fact(5))` now executes cleanly and prints `120`. Original panic at `state.rs:1300` is gone.

Both tests now pass cleanly.

## Report

**Root cause:** `singlevaraux` in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:1769` had a `TODO(port)` stub that did NOT recurse into the enclosing `FuncState` chain. When a name (e.g. `fact` referenced inside its own body) wasn't found as a local or existing upvalue at the current function level, the code fell through to `Void` (global), so the inner closure never registered the upvalue. The bytecode emitter still wrote a `GETUPVAL` for upvalue 0 referencing `fact`, but the runtime closure was constructed with `upvals.len() == 0` (because `proto.upvalues.len() == 0`) — `cl.upvals[0]` then panicked at `state.rs:1300`. Also `singlevar` duplicated the same broken single-level lookup instead of calling the recursive helper.

**Fix:** Restructured `singlevaraux` to properly recurse via `fs.prev.as_deref_mut()` — when the name is not local and not an existing upvalue at the current level, it walks up the parent chain, and on unwinding calls `new_upvalue` at each level if the parent had it as a local or upvalue, mirroring C `lparser.c`'s `singlevaraux`. Rewrote `singlevar` to `take()` `ls.fs` out, call the recursive helper, then put it back — sidestepping the previously-flagged `&LexState` / `&mut FuncState` borrow conflict.

**New status:** `print(fact(5))` now executes cleanly and prints `120`. Original panic at `state.rs:1300` is gone.
